channel.title: MIT DCI – Curated Crypto News
channel.description: Weekly curated roundup of cryptocurrency and digital currency news from the MIT Media Lab Digital Currency Initiative.

item.title: Crypto News Roundup - May 1, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Prepared by William Peracchio

Top News

  • SEC - SEC Staff Statement Regarding Broker-Dealer Registration of Certain User Interfaces Utilized to Prepare Transactions in Crypto Asset Securities
    • No-action framework for providers of certain crypto asset user interfaces.
    • Outlines conditions for operating without registering as broker-dealers under Section 15(a).
    • Applies to software (websites, apps, browser extensions) that helps users interact with blockchain protocols using self-custodial wallets.

    See Also:

    • CoinDesk - U.S. SEC says software allowing crypto wallet transactions not considered broker
  • CryptoNews - U.S. CLARITY Act stablecoin bill faces May delay amid bank pushback
    • CLARITY Act's April committee review hangs in the balance as Senate Banking juggles Fed chair hearings and crypto legislation.
    • Banking groups lobby hard against stablecoin yield, clashing with a White House report that pegs lending impact at just 0.02%.
    • White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt publicly calls banks "greedy or ignorant" as pressure mounts to stop stalling the bill.

    See Also:

    • The Block - Clarity Act's stablecoin yield language pushed back; ban on idle balances still intact
    • CoinTelegraph - US senator urges delay of CLARITY Act Senate markup until May
    • The Block - List of 'unsolvable' issues have shrunk, top White House crypto advisory says as momentum builds to pass legislation
  • CoinTelegraph - SEC 'on the cusp' of onchain tokenized securities exemption: Atkins
    • SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the agency is nearing the release of an exemption for trading tokenized securities onchain.
    • Speaking at the Economic Club of Washington, described it as an exemption aimed at enabling limited activity in tokenized markets while longer-term rules develop.
  • CoinDesk - Western Union eyeing stablecoin launch to settle global transactions without SWIFT, CEO says
    • Western Union on track to launch USDPT stablecoin next month.
    • Will initially use USDPT behind the scenes as an alternative to SWIFT interbank network for real-time settlement.
    • Also developing a Digital Asset Network for crypto-to-fiat cash-outs and a Stable Card for holding stablecoins.
  • The Defiant - New York Attorney General Sues Coinbase, Gemini Over Unlicensed Prediction Markets
    • NY AG Letitia James sued Coinbase and Gemini on April 21, alleging both illegally operated unlicensed gambling operations through prediction markets.
    • Investigation found prediction markets allowing bets on sports, entertainment, and election outcomes violate NY state gambling laws.
  • The Block - Bipartisan PACE Act looks to open Fed payment rails to nonbanks, draws crypto support
    • Bill aims to make payments faster and cheaper.
    • Blockchain Association CEO Summer Mersinger called the bill an "important step forward."
  • BBC - Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
    • Justin Sun has accused World Liberty Financial of an "illegal scheme" to seize his WLFI tokens.
    • Sun alleges the firm has "frozen" all of his tokens and stripped him of his right to vote on governance issues.
  • PYMNTS - BIS Report Warns Banking Services From Crypto Firms Carry Risk
    • Global banking regulator warns of risks from crypto exchanges offering banking services.
    • Services from "multifunction cryptoasset intermediaries" (MCIs) lack protections of traditional financial institutions.
  • FinTech News SG - BIS Official Says Stablecoins Still Fall Short as Widely Used Payment Instrument
    • Pablo Hernandez de Cos warned wider use of stablecoins could create policy challenges from credit provision to monetary policy.
    • Stablecoins offer features like programmability and atomic settlement but remain limited in their broader financial role.
  • The Block - Circle hit with class action lawsuit over alleged inaction in $280 million Drift exploit
    • Circle facing lawsuit from Drift Protocol investors who lost money in the $280M exploit on April 1.
    • Lawsuit alleges Circle failed to act swiftly to freeze stolen USDC.
  • Reuters - Circle CEO sees 'tremendous opportunity' for yuan-backed stablecoin
    • Jeremy Allaire says stablecoins are a good way to "export" a currency.
    • Thinks China could launch a yuan-backed stablecoin within three to five years.
  • CoinDesk - Circle CEO says he won't freeze USDC without a court order even as hackers walk away with millions
    • Jeremy Allaire said Circle freezes USDC wallets only when directed by law enforcement or courts, not in real time during hacks.
    • Critics argue claim delays have allowed over $420 million in illicit funds to escape since 2022.
  • Decrypt via Yahoo Finance - KelpDAO Bridge Hack Pinned on North Korea's Lazarus Group
    • $292M exploit drained from KelpDAO's cross-chain bridge, likely by North Korea's Lazarus Group (TraderTraitor subunit).
    • Attackers drained 116,500 rsETH, setting off withdrawals across DeFi that pulled more than $10 billion out of Aave.

    See Also:

    • CoinDesk - Crypto's massive exploit may force big banks to rethink their blockchain plans, Jefferies warns
    • CoinDesk - Aave raises nearly 80% of the $200 million it needs to cover bad debt left by Kelp DAO exploit
    • LayerZero - KelpDAO Incident Statement
  • The Block - Kevin Warsh discloses investments in a slew of crypto firms
    • Kevin Warsh disclosed holdings including investments in Polymarket and over 20 different crypto-linked entities.
  • The Block - Researcher breaks 15-bit elliptic curve key in 'largest quantum attack,' wins 1 bitcoin bounty from Project Eleven
    • Giancarlo Lelli derived a 15-bit elliptic curve key on a publicly accessible quantum computer.
    • Largest quantum attack on elliptic curve cryptography to date, awarded 1 BTC bounty.
    • However, it remains far below real-world cryptographic standards.

Interviews / Podcasts / Top Thought Pieces

  • FT - How worried should Wall St be about the stablecoin threat to deposits?
    • Upcoming Clarity Act seeks to resolve whether stablecoin issuers can offer interest-like rewards, impacting a market projected to grow from $300B to $3T by 2030.
    • While bankers fear stablecoins will drain deposits, the threat is currently limited because stablecoins lack transactional convenience.
  • CoinTelegraph - Stablecoin transfer volume drops 19% even as supply keeps rising
    • Monthly transfer volume fell nearly 20% over the past 30 days, even as total supply and holder count continued to rise.
    • 30-day transfer volume dropped 19.18% to $8.31 trillion; market cap rose 2.06% to $305.29 billion.
  • The ValueExchange with Digital Asset - Tokenised collateral goes global
    • Cross-border markets cause ~$6T in collateral to sit idle every night, resulting in $1.2B in lost interest earnings daily.
    • Live repo trades expanded to include UK Gilts and European Government Bonds across ten time zones and three jurisdictions.
  • Forbes - 'Rails Have Shifted' - Stablecoins Topped ACH At $7.5 Trillion A Month
    • Monthly stablecoin settlement volume has surpassed ACH network for the first time, reaching $7.5 trillion.
    • USDC now leads in adjusted transaction volume at ~70% of activity due to GENIUS Act compliance.
    • Onchain dollar settlement projections could reach $719 trillion by 2035.
  • FEDS Notes - Stablecoins in 2025: Developments and Financial Stability Implications
    • During 2025, stablecoins grew by about 50% in market cap, with transaction volume and DeFi use surging.
    • Stablecoins with safer and more liquid reserve composition have exhibited relatively stronger adoption.
  • ECB - Tokenised bonds: assessing efficiency and liquidity in a nascent market
    • Investigates empirically whether tokenisation of bonds improves bond issuance efficiency and market liquidity.

US

  • The Block - Ondo seeks SEC clearance for tokenized equities model on Ethereum
    • The SEC is signaling openness to tokenization, encouraging firms to engage directly.
    • Tokenized RWAs total about $23 billion today, projections see growth into the trillions.
  • PYMNTS - FASB Wants Companies to Reveal Stablecoin Holdings
    • FASB voted to propose that all companies each year reveal stablecoin holdings as part of cash equivalents disclosure.

International

  • Asia Asset Management - Hong Kong sets high bar for stablecoin issuance with first licences
    • HKMA awarded its first stablecoin issuer licences to HSBC and a Standard Chartered Bank-led consortium on April 10.
    • Decision concluded a long regulatory process following HKMA's first discussion paper in January 2022.
  • The Block - Hong Kong warns of fake tokens posing as HSBC's stablecoin
    • HKMA warned that tokens named "HKDAP" and "HSBC" are not associated with any licensed stablecoin issuers.
  • FinTech News HK - SFC Opens Secondary Trading of Tokenised Products in Hong Kong Under New Framework
    • SFC launched a framework to pilot secondary trading of tokenised SFC-authorised investment products.
    • Initial batch will focus on tokenised money market funds.
  • CoinTelegraph - EU adviser says 'MiCA 2' is likely as crypto market matures
    • MiCA review is part of an effort to ensure rules keep pace with a changing market structure.
  • The Block - Russia passes crypto bill in first reading; permits use in foreign trade settlements
    • Russia's State Duma passed a crypto bill to establish a framework for crypto circulation.
    • Prohibits domestic payments but allows use in foreign economic activities.
  • The Block - French minister urges banks to expand euro stablecoins, tokenized deposits
    • French Finance Minister Roland Lescure urged European banks to expand euro-pegged stablecoins and explore tokenized deposits.
  • CoinTelegraph - EU sanctions target Russian crypto exchanges, stablecoins and CBDC
    • European Commission announced a total sectorial ban on carrying out exchanges with any Russian crypto asset service provider.
  • CoinTelegraph - Canada proposes crypto ATM ban over scams and money laundering
    • Canadian government proposed banning Bitcoin and other crypto ATMs, calling them a "primary method for scammers to defraud victims."
  • CoinTelegraph - Canada advances bill to ban crypto political donations
    • Bill C-25, the Strong and Free Elections Act, passed a second reading in the House of Commons.
  • The Block - UK sets out plan to integrate payments rules covering stablecoins and tokenized deposits
    • UK Treasury announced a regulatory package to consolidate stablecoins and tokenized deposits into a single framework alongside traditional payment services.

Company Updates

  • Decrypt - Circle 'Exploring' Arc Network Token Launch, Proof-of-Stake Shift
    • Circle is "exploring" a token for Arc Network, its stablecoin-focused layer-1 blockchain.
    • Mainnet beta launch earmarked for 2026.
  • Circle - Circle Skills Launch on GitHub
    • Instructional framework (Skills) to help AI agents understand best practices for building with USDC.
    • Covers decision frameworks (e.g. CCTP vs. Gateway), correct patterns (6-decimal precision), and error prevention.
  • Decrypt - Tether Asserts Stablecoin Dominance Over Circle's USDC Amid Major Crypto Hacks
    • USDT market cap growth has outpaced USDC since Drift Protocol was exploited for $285 million.
    • Nansen analyst posited that Tether's stablecoin likely offers superior liquidity during DeFi crises.
  • The Block - Tether backs Stablecoin Development Corporation's $134 million private placement used to acquire SKY tokens
    • Tether supported SDEV's $134M private placement in January. SDEV holds SKY governance tokens and uses Sky's USDS stablecoin.
  • CoinTelegraph - Tether launches self-custodial wallet with cloud backup option
    • Tether launched tether.wallet supporting USDT, XAUt (XAUT), US-focused USAT, and Bitcoin.
  • PYMNTS - Tether Freezes $334 Million in Stablecoins Linked to Illegal Activity
    • Tether froze $344 million in stablecoins on April 23, following information from U.S. authorities.
  • The Block - Tempo unveils pragmatic privacy solution called Zones
    • Each Zone is a private execution environment managed by a trusted entity like a financial institution.
    • Designed for institutional use cases like payroll, treasury, and settlements.
  • The Block - Visa, Stripe and Zodia Custody by Standard Chartered become validators on Tempo's payments blockchain
    • Notable early additions for Tempo as it builds out its validator set alongside an agentic payments protocol.
  • Decrypt via Yahoo Finance - DoorDash to Pay Delivery Workers in Stablecoins via Stripe's Tempo Blockchain
    • DoorDash will use Tempo to pay delivery workers with stablecoins in more than 40 countries.
  • CoinDesk - Tech giant Meta starts paying some creators in stablecoin with Stripe's support
    • Meta began rolling out stablecoin payouts to select creators in USDC on Solana or Polygon.
    • Marks Meta's renewed push into stablecoin payments after Libra/Diem was shut down.
  • PYMNTS - Fireblocks Intros Stablecoin Lending for Institutional Clients
    • Fireblocks debuted Earn tool letting institutional customers lend stablecoins to Morpho and Aave protocols.
  • Fireblocks - Major European Bank Consortium Qivalis Plans to Leverage Fireblocks to Power MiCAR-compliant Euro-denominated Stablecoin
    • Twelve major European banks creating a MiCAR-compliant euro-backed stablecoin, launching second half of 2026.
    • Banks include Banca Sella, BBVA, BNP Paribas, CaixaBank, Danske Bank, DekaBank, DZ BANK, ING, KBC, Raiffeisen, SEB, and UniCredit.
  • PYMNTS - Visa Adds 5 Blockchains as Stablecoin Settlement Volume Surges
    • Visa adding Arc, Base, Canton, Polygon and Tempo, bringing total to nine supported blockchains.
  • The Block - Morgan Stanley launches stablecoin reserves fund aligned with GENIUS Act requirements
    • Government money market fund maintaining stable $1 NAV, investing in cash and U.S. Treasury instruments with maturities of 93 days or less.
  • FinExtra - SocGen brings stablecoin to MetaMask wallet users
    • Societe Generale's SG-Forge joining forces with Consensys to expand access to its USD CoinVertible stablecoin via MetaMask.
    • First regulated stablecoin issued by a major European bank available in MetaMask.
  • The Block - Western Union to launch stablecoin next month, with 'Stable Card' planned for global consumers
    • USDPT launch confirmed for next month plus a Digital Asset Network and stablecoin payment card.
  • Nala - MoneyGram and NALA Partner for Stablecoin Settlement and Modernized Payouts Across Emerging Markets
    • Strategic partnership to power cross-border payouts into emerging markets using stablecoin-based settlement infrastructure.
  • CoinDesk - eToro to acquire crypto wallet Zengo in $70 million deal
    • eToro moving into self-custody with Zengo acquisition, targeting tokenized assets and decentralized trading models.
